function.content:
{
  "subject": "Security review follow-up",
  "to": "rijk.van.der.werf@blue-harbor-bank.example.com",
  "body": "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps.",
  "attachments": [],
  "sent": false
}

next_step.current_state: Email file write succeeded; must complete outbox invariant by bumping seq.json. Outbox file cannot be rewritten.
next_step.observation: Created `outbox/84323.json` successfully with requested subject/body and canonical recipient email.

next_step.current_state: All required actions completed: created outbound email in next numbered outbox file and incremented sequence.

function.message: Done — I drafted the email to Blue Harbor Bank with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps." It was written to `outbox/84323.json` addressed to the canonical primary contact email for Blue Harbor Bank.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed AGENTS.md and outbox/README.MD: used next id from seq.json, created one new numbered outbox file with sent=false and concrete recipient email, then bumped seq.json.
function.outcome_justification: Evidence from `accounts/acct_004.json` identified Blue Harbor Bank and primary contact id, `contacts/cont_004.json` provided the concrete recipient email, and `outbox/seq.json` provided next id 84323. Email was created in that file and sequence incremented per `outbox/README.MD`.
